metadata.description: Potato crop calendar for Kenya highlands (1,500-3,000m ASL), derived from NPCK and CRAFT literature
sources: Climate Resilient Potato Production Handbook - NPCK & CRAFT
notes: Calendar is for Kenya's highland potato zone (1,500-3,000m ASL)
Two crops per year aligned to bimodal rainfall
Crop duration: 120-150 days depending on variety
Timing shifts by ±2 weeks depending on altitude and rainfall onset

short_rains.label: Short Rains Season (secondary)
short_rains.note: Lower yields currently but improving under climate change

land_preparation.description: Plough 15-25cm deep, harrow, prepare furrows/ridges. Apply lime if pH <6.0. Apply well-decomposed manure.

planting.description: Plant well-sprouted seed (3+ sprouts) at 30cm spacing, 15cm depth. Apply DAP fertilizer at planting.

emergence.description: Shoots emerge through soil. First weeding begins 2-3 weeks after emergence. Cutworm scouting critical.

vegetative.description: Rapid canopy growth. First and second hilling. Begin fungicide spray (Mancozeb every 7 days). Aphid monitoring.

tuberization.description: Tuber initiation. Soil moisture CRITICAL to prevent common scab. Optimal soil temp 15-24°C. 70% of potassium uptake in first 6 weeks.

critical_conditions.soil_moisture: must maintain — dry soil causes scab

bulking.description: Tubers enlarge rapidly. Highest nitrogen demand — top-dress if deficient. PTM risk if tubers exposed. Continue fungicide program.

maturation.description: Foliage yellows. Dehaulm when ≥50% haulms yellow (100-120 DAE). Leave tubers 2 weeks for skin to harden.
key_activities: Dehaulm at 100-120 DAE when 50%+ haulms yellow
Leave tubers in ground 2 weeks after dehaulming for skin set

harvest.description: Harvest only in dry weather with cloud cover. Thumb test: skin should not slip. Move tubers to shade within 3 hours.

curing.description: Wound healing before storage. Temperature 18-20°C, humidity 85-90% RH, for 10-14 days. Well-ventilated shed.

harvest.action: Short rains crop ready to harvest. Harvest only in dry weather with cloud cover. Confirm skin set (thumb test).

pest_monitoring.action: PTM risk high in warm dry conditions. Check ridges for exposed tubers. Inspect stored tubers for PTM damage.

spraying.action: Short rains crop at maturation/harvest — stop fungicide 2 weeks before harvest.

planting.action: Prepare land for long rains planting. Plough during dry season. Take soil samples (dry soil only).

harvest.action: Complete short rains harvest. Cure tubers at 18-20°C, 85-90% RH for 10-14 days.

planting.action: Land preparation for long rains. Plough 15-25cm. Harrow 2 weeks later. Apply lime if pH <6.0. Apply manure.

spraying.action: No crop in field. Clean and fumigate storage.

planting.action: PLANT NOW at rain onset. Use well-sprouted seed (3+ sprouts). 30cm spacing, 15cm depth. Apply DAP at planting.

spraying.action: Begin Mancozeb fungicide from crop emergence. Spray every 7 days. First insecticide at 10 days after germination.

pest_monitoring.action: Set up 6 yellow water traps per acre for aphids. Scout for cutworms at emergence (check early morning for cut stems).

irrigation.action: Rains provide moisture. Ensure drainage is clear.

spraying.action: Continue Mancozeb every 7 days — peak blight risk. Alternate with Metalaxyl. Do NOT skip sprays.

fertilizer.action: First hilling + weeding when crop is 20cm high. CAN top-dress if nitrogen deficiency visible.

pest_monitoring.action: Check aphid traps weekly. Spray Alphacypermethrin if detected. Scout for cutworm damage.

spraying.action: Continue fungicide every 7 days. Late blight risk remains very high.

irrigation.action: Soil moisture critical at tuber set — irrigate if dry spell occurs. Dry soil at tuber set causes common scab.

fertilizer.action: Second hilling 2-3 weeks after first. Top-dress N before bulking phase if deficient.

pest_monitoring.action: As rains ease, aphid risk rises. Check tubers are covered — PTM risk increases as ridges erode.

spraying.action: Continue fungicide if canopy still green. Reduce frequency as foliage yellows.

harvest.action: Early varieties may be ready. Dehaulm when ≥50% haulms yellow. Leave 2 weeks for skin set.

pest_monitoring.action: PTM risk HIGH — warm dry conditions. Check ridges, re-earth up if tubers exposed. Cut foliage at 80 DAP.

harvest.action: MAIN HARVEST. Harvest only in dry weather with cloud cover. Thumb test for skin set. Move to shade within 3 hours.

pest_monitoring.action: PTM very active. Harvest immediately after dehaulming — do not leave tubers in ground. Fumigate store before use.

harvest.action: Late varieties still being harvested. Cure tubers 10-14 days at 18-20°C, 85-90% RH.

planting.action: Begin preparing for short rains. Procure certified seed. Check DLS for aphid infestation on stored seed.
Land preparation for short rains. Plough 15-25cm. Apply lime if needed. Apply manure. Harrow and prepare ridges.

pest_monitoring.action: Check stored seed for PTM and aphids. Remove apical sprout 10-14 days before planting to get 3+ sprouts.

planting.action: PLANT at rain onset. Use well-sprouted seed. Same protocol as March planting.

spraying.action: Begin Mancozeb at emergence. Every 7 days. First insecticide at 10 DAP.

pest_monitoring.action: Set up yellow water traps. Scout for cutworms at emergence.

spraying.action: Mancozeb every 7 days. Peak blight risk. Do NOT skip.

fertilizer.action: First hilling + weeding at 20cm height. CAN top-dress if needed.

pest_monitoring.action: Check aphid traps. Scout for cutworm damage.

irrigation.action: If rains easing, irrigate at tuber set. Dry soil = common scab risk.

pest_monitoring.action: Aphid risk rising as rains ease. PTM check on ridges. Second hilling if not done.

early.examples: Use for shortened seasons or late planting

late.examples: For full-length seasons with reliable rainfall
